note: Regenerate with `python3 scripts/discover_runtime_capabilities.py`. Versions below were detected on the bootstrap host; discovery re-detects at runtime.

runtimes.supported_invocation_mechanism: slash commands + skills (Skill tool) + subagents (Agent/Task tools)
detected_limitations: Long procedures should live in skills, not CLAUDE.md.
Hooks are shell/CLI scripts wired via settings.json.

supported_commands: natural-language entrypoint: 'Implement the active system plan using the implement-max skill.'
runtimes.supported_invocation_mechanism: AGENTS.md instructions + config.toml + skills + validation scripts
detected_limitations: No native event-hook system equivalent to Claude PreToolUse/PostToolUse.
Hook-like behavior is implemented via wrapper scripts, skills, task gates, and CI.
Codex reads AGENTS.md as its primary instruction file.
compatibility_decisions: Secret-exposure blocking: Claude uses a PreToolUse hook; Codex uses scan_secrets.py as a mandatory task gate + CI check.
Phase logging: Claude uses SubagentStop/Stop hooks; Codex uses phase-completion-reporting skill + execution-ledger writes.
